pdg_node.informal_desc: Loads serialized model data and exposes structural sizing metadata for downstream computation.

declaration.name: modelspecloadingandsizing
declaration.type_signature: (filename: str) -> tuple[ModelDSpec, float]
declaration.docstring: Loads serialized model data and exposes structural sizing metadata for downstream computation.
declaration.conceptual_summary: SerializedStructureIngestionAndComplexityRatioExtraction. Reads a serialized structured artifact from a valid filesystem path and deserializes it into a typed in-memory specification for computation. It then derives a normalized structural complexity metric by computing the ratio between discrete state cardinality and representational dimensionality. The operation maps persistent encoded data into both an executable structural object and a scalar size invariant.. Applications: Finite-element engineering: load a serialized solver/mesh specification and compute state-variable density per geometric dimension., Robotics autonomy: ingest a serialized transition-system model and compute state-cardinality per latent-coordinate dimension., Computational biology: parse a serialized hidden-state model and compute hidden-state count per observation-space dimension., Network science: load a serialized dynamical graph-process specification and compute process-state count per embedding dimension.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Synthesizes control action from current state and derivative information.

declaration.name: controlinputsynthesis
declaration.type_signature: (_x: array-like, _x_dot: array-like, _t: float) -> array-like
declaration.docstring: Synthesizes control action from current state and derivative information.
declaration.conceptual_summary: State-Tangent-to-Input Projection. Computes a vector-valued input by applying a synthesis map to an instantaneous state vector, its first-order time derivative estimate, and a scalar temporal coordinate. The transform converts local phase-space information (position in state space plus tangent direction) into an admissible excitation vector for the governed dynamics. This is a causal per-step feedback projection that may be linear or nonlinear and may vary with time.. Applications: Trajectory stabilization in autonomous aerial navigation using state and rate estimates, Infusion-rate computation in physiological regulation models from concentration states and slopes, Coil-current command synthesis in electromagnetic plasma-shaping systems, Thermal actuator drive generation in semiconductor process chambers from temperature fields and gradients
